hints.Ipse: 'Ipse' is an intensive pronoun meaning 'he himself'
hints.vos: 'vos' is a personal pronoun meaning 'you (plural)' — the direct object
hints.Spiritu: 'Spiritu' is a noun meaning 'Spirit' in the ablative

hints.to dip: 'to dip' is 'tingere/mergere' — 'baptizare' is a Latinized Greek loanword meaning 'to baptize'

hints.Present: Present would be 'baptizat' — 'baptizabit' with -bi- is future
hints.Imperfect: Imperfect would be 'baptizabat' — 'baptizabit' is future active

hints.lavo, lavare, lavi, lautum: These are the parts of 'lavare' (to wash)
hints.mergo, mergere, mersi, mersum: These are the parts of 'mergere' (to plunge/dip)
hints.tingo, tingere, tinxi, tinctum: These are the parts of 'tingere' (to dip/dye)

hints.Nominative: Nominative would be 'Spiritus' — 'Spiritu' with -u ending is ablative (4th declension)
hints.Dative: Dative would be 'Spiritui' — 'Spiritu' is ablative of means
hints.Genitive: Genitive would be 'Spiritus' — 'Spiritu' is ablative singular

hints.1st declension: 1st declension has genitive in -ae — 'spiritus' genitive ends in -ūs
hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension has genitive in -i — 'spiritus' genitive ends in -ūs
hints.3rd declension: 3rd declension has genitive in -is — 'spiritus' genitive ends in -ūs

hints.Nominative: Nominative would be 'Sanctus' — 'Sancto' is ablative agreeing with 'Spiritu'
hints.Dative: 'Sancto' could be dative, but here it agrees with 'Spiritu' in the ablative
hints.Genitive: Genitive would be 'Sancti' — 'Sancto' is ablative modifying 'Spiritu'

hints.The Spirit: The Spirit is the instrument (ablative) — 'Ipse' refers to the one who is coming after John
hints.John the Baptist: John is the speaker — 'Ipse' refers to the one mightier than John, i.e., Christ
hints.God the Father: 'Ipse' refers to the one coming after John — Christ, not the Father directly

translation: He himself will baptize you with the Holy Spirit
